name: Cranberry Cake Wreath
recipeIngredient: Cinnamon red candies or cranberries for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 12-cup ring mold or Bundt pan.
2. In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Make a well in the center. Add eggs, orange juice, and butter to the well; stir liquid ingredients just until blended.
3. Gradually add cranberries and walnuts to the well. Stir together liquid and dry ingredients just until combined. Pour batter into the prepared pan.
4. Bake in the preheated 350°F oven for 45 to 50 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow cooling for 10 minutes, then remove the wreath from the pan and cool completely.
5. Prepare the glaze by gradually stirring 3 to 4 teaspoons of milk into 2 cups of powdered sugar (confectioners' sugar) in a small bowl until smooth and at a good glazing consistency. Spread 1 cup of glaze over the top of the cooled cake, letting it run down the sides. Tint the remaining glaze with green food coloring if desired.
6. Pipe the tinted glaze onto pine branches as pictured, and decorate with cinnamon red candies or cranberries and a red bow, if desired.
